subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares; describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc.; and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into equal parts like halves, thirds, or fourths, name these parts, and understand that equal parts can look different.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into rows and columns of equal squares and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or sides. Identify triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons and cubes (identify number of faces).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw shapes with a certain number of sides or angles, like triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes, and know how many faces a cube has.

subject: Math
description: Create a pictograph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put together, take-apart and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can make simple pictographs and bar graphs with up to four categories and use them to solve basic addition, subtraction, and comparison problems.

subject: Math
description: Show the measurements by making a dot plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can show measurement data by making a dot plot with whole-number units on the horizontal scale.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit or measure the same object multiple times to collect data.

subject: Math
description: Identify a statistical question focused on measurements.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that focus on measurements.

subject: Math
description: Investigate questions involving measurements.
description_plain: Your child can explore and answer questions that involve measuring things.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems with adding and subtracting within 100, (not using dollars and cents simultaneously) using the $ and ¢ symbols appropriately (not including decimal notation).
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting numbers up to 100, using $ and ¢ symbols correctly but not decimals.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes on both analog and digital clocks, using a.m. and p.m.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, … and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can show whole numbers on a number line starting at zero and use the number line to add and subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about lengths under 100 by adding or subtracting, using drawings and equations with a missing number.

subject: Math
description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of either a customary or metric standard length unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much longer one object is than another using inches, feet, centimeters, or meters.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, yards, centimeters and meters.
description_plain: Your child can guess the length of objects using inches, feet, yards, centimeters, or meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure an object two times using different units and explain how the measurements compare.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks and measuring tapes.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is using tools like rulers, yardsticks, or measuring tapes.

subject: Math
description: Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can explain why addition and subtraction methods work by understanding place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100–900 and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number 100–900.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add or subtract 10 or 100 to numbers between 100 and 900 in their head.

subject: Math
description: Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.
description_plain: Your child understands that when adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, they add or subtract hundreds, tens, and ones separately, sometimes regrouping tens or hundreds.

subject: Math
description:
Represent and solve addition and subtraction problems using…
  • concrete models or drawings;
  • strategies based on place value;
  • properties of operations;
  • the relationship between addition and subtraction and;
  • relate drawings and strategies to expressions or equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems using objects, drawings, place value ideas, math rules, and write equations to show their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add up to four two-digit numbers using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 100 using place value, math rules, or knowing how addition and subtraction relate.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens and ones digits, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers by looking at hundreds, tens, and ones and use >, =, or < symbols to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers to 1000 using base-ten numerals, number names and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 1000 in regular form, word form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Count forwards and backwards within 1000; skip-count by 5s, 10s and 100s.
description_plain: Your child can count forward and backward up to 1000 and skip count by 5s, 10s, and 100s.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine hundreds (and 0 tens and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers like 100, 200, 300, and so on mean one, two, three, etc. hundreds with zero tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: 100 can be thought of as a bundle of ten tens — called a "hundred."
description_plain: Your child understands that 100 is a group of ten tens, called a 'hundred.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens and ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that a three-digit number shows how many hundreds, tens, and ones it has.

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rows and columns up to 5 by 5 and write an equation showing equal groups added together.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number and write an equation showing an even number as two equal groups added together.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers within 20 in their head.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step word problems with addition and subtraction up to 100 by using drawings and equations with a missing number.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas that similar words express.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including but not limited to irony, in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figurative language, like irony, by looking at how it is used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word connections, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words to help understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation and determine or clarity the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses books and online tools to find how to say words and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of words from Greek and Latin to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child finds the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action, expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use different verb forms to focus on who is doing the action or to show uncertainty or imaginary situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of strategies and resources (print and electronic) to identify and correct spelling errors.
description_plain: Your child uses tools and strategies to find and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child uses ellipses correctly to show when words are left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of punctuation to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child uses punctuation properly to show pauses or breaks in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional and subjunctive mood, while recognizing and correcting inappropriate shifts.
description_plain: Your child uses different types of verbs correctly and fixes mistakes when verb forms change wrongly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in both active and passive voice correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify verbals correctly based on their intended function.
description_plain: Your child can identify verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives based on how they are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: In both written and oral expression:
description_plain: Your child can express ideas clearly in both speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose routinely over extended time frames and shorter time frames for a variety of tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, reasons, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and, in order to reflect, analyze or complete short re-search projects, quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism by providing in-text and bibliographic MLA or APA citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it correctly in projects by quoting or rewording with proper credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating new avenues for inquiry.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and thinking of new questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital resources to create and publish products as well as to interact and collaborate with others; cite sources using MLA or APA format.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools to create and share work, work with others, and give credit to sources using MLA or APA style.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying different ways to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that connects the narrative's relevance to the intended purpose of the writing.
description_plain: Your child can write endings that explain why the story or writing matters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and detailed words that help show actions and feelings in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitions to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use different words to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description and reflection, to develop experiences, events and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use storytelling tools like dialogue and description to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage the reader by setting out a problem, situation or observation, establishing a point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; create a smooth progression of experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by introducing a problem or situation, point of view, and characters, and keep the story flowing smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized, using a style that fits the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narratives strategically in other modes of writing, utilizing effective technique, well-chosen details and well-structured sequences for an intended purpose.
description_plain: Your child can use stories in other types of writing by choosing good details and organizing events well for a clear purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, re-writing or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, or editing, focusing on how well it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows the information they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal tone in their writing when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different linking words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, and examples that fit well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly; organize ideas, concepts and information into broader categories; include formatting, graphics and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize ideas into groups, and use pictures or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative and/or explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts and information through the selection, organization and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative or explanatory texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, re-writing or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that supports their main argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a task appropriate writing style.
description_plain: Your child can keep a writing style that fits the task they are working on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases that connect ideas and make their reasons and evidence clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with clear reasons and facts from trustworthy sources and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish opposing claim(s) and counter/refute them, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their opinion, recognize opposing views, explain why they disagree, and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, synthesizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend, and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can use different reading strategies to understand and analyze difficult informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze two or more texts with conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree in fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts about the same topic and find where they disagree in facts or opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can find the main argument and claims in a text and decide if the reasons and evidence are strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using print and non-print formats for presenting particular topics or ideas.
description_plain: Your child can think about the pros and cons of using books, videos, or other formats to share information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective and purpose in a text, and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view and purpose, and see how the author deals with opposing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a paragraph is organized and how its sentences help develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative or technical meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author uses comparisons, analogies or categories to make connections among and distinctions between ideas over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses comparisons or categories to connect or separate ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas of a text, and analyze how they are developed through relationships of key details, citing textual evidence, paraphrasing or summarizing.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a text and explain how details support those ideas by quoting or summarizing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important details from a text to explain what it says directly and what it suggests.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, synthesizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex literary texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can use different reading strategies to understand and analyze difficult stories and literary texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events or character types from myths, traditional stories or religious works, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a modern story uses ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional stories, or religious texts like the Bible, and show how the story makes these ideas new.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed/live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or play version of a story to the original text and explain how the director or actors made choices that are similar or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze characters' and readers' perspectives and how the differences create effects, including but not limited to suspense, humor and empathy.
description_plain: Your child can understand how characters and readers see things differently and how this creates feelings like suspense, humor, or empathy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast the structure of two or more texts, and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized and explain how their different structures affect their meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including but not limited to analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a story, including figurative language, and explain how word choices affect the mood or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how specific dialogue or events in a story move the plot forward, show character traits, or cause decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine themes of a text, and analyze how they are developed through relationships of characters, setting and plot, citing textual evidence, paraphrasing or summarizing.
description_plain: Your child can find the main themes in a story and explain how characters, setting, and plot help develop those themes using examples from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important parts of a text to explain what it clearly says and what can be understood by reading between the lines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an understanding of verbs and adjectives and their synonyms.
description_plain: Your child understands verbs and adjectives and can recognize words that mean the same.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an understanding of verbs and adjectives and their antonyms.
description_plain: Your child understands verbs and adjectives and can recognize words that mean the opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can group everyday objects, like shapes or foods, to understand what each group means.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child explores how words are related and what different words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify common affixes and how they change the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can find common beginnings or endings of words and explain how they change the word’s meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words based on what they read or hear in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child spells simple words by sounding them out using what they know about letters and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child can write letters for most consonant and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize the first word in a sentence and the word I.

subject: Language Arts
description: sentences using common prepositions.
description_plain: Your child can say and write sentences using common words that show location or direction, like 'in,' 'on,' or 'under.'

subject: Language Arts
description: interrogative sentences using who, what, where, when, why and how.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions using words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: regular plural nouns by orally adding /s/ or /es/.
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding sounds like /s/ or /es/ out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: When writing or speaking, demonstrate appropriate use of:
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar when speaking or writing, including making plurals, asking questions, and using prepositions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, collect information from real-world experiences or provided sources to answer or generate questions.
description_plain: With help, your child can gather information from real life or other sources to answer or ask questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: With help, your child can take part in group research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital resources to create and publish products, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can explore digital tools to create and share work, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, change, and improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include details which describe actions, thoughts, emotions.
description_plain: Your child can add details about actions, thoughts, and feelings in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child can write in a way that fits the purpose and topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives, using a combination of drawing, dictating, writing and digital resources, to develop real or imagined experiences or multiple events or ideas, using effective technique, descriptive de-tails and clear sequences.
description_plain: Your child can create stories or descriptions by drawing, speaking, writing, or using digital tools to share real or imagined events with clear details and order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, changing, and fixing it with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions to develop text structure across paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child uses simple words to connect ideas between paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate conjunctions to develop text structure within sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and' or 'but' to connect ideas within sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Supply information to develop the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds information to explain the topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, strengthen writing through peer collaboration and adding details through writing and/or pictures as needed.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child makes their writing better by working together and adding details with words or pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative and/or explanatory texts, using a combination of drawing, dictating, writing and digital resources, to establish a topic and supply information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child creates informative or explanatory writing using drawing, speaking, writing, or digital tools to share a topic and facts about it.
