subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital resources to create and publish products, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child can use computers or tablets, with help, to create and share work, sometimes with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can plan, change, and improve their writing with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include details which describe actions, thoughts, emotions.
description_plain: Your child can add details about actions, feelings, and thoughts in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount a single event or multiple events, memories or ideas.
description_plain: Your child can tell about one or more events, memories, or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can write stories or reports that fit the task and purpose, with help from adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives, using writing and digital resources, to develop real or imagined experiences or multiple events or ideas, using effective technique, descriptive details and clear sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories or use digital tools to share real or imagined events with clear details and order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions to develop text structure across paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child can use words that connect ideas between paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate conjunctions to develop text structure within sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' or 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Supply information with detail to develop the topic.
description_plain: Your child can add details to explain their topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, strengthen writing through peer collaboration and adding details through writing and/or pictures as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with others and add details or pictures to make their writing stronger.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative and/or explanatory texts, using writing and digital resources, to establish a topic and provide information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can write reports or explanations using writing and digital tools to share information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions.
description_plain: Your child can use words that help connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons with details to support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons and details to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic, followed by opinion statement, and create an organizational structure.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, share their opinion, and organize their writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, strengthen writing through peer collaboration and adding details through writing and/or pictures as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with others and use writing or pictures to add details and make their writing better with some help from adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose opinion pieces, using a combination of writing and digital resources, on topics or texts, with supporting reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces about topics or books, using writing and digital tools, and give reasons to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce formation of all upper- and lowercase cursive letters.
description_plain: Your child will learn how to write all uppercase and lowercase letters in cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use different reading strategies like asking questions and summarizing to understand and think about grade-level nonfiction books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between information from two or more texts on the same theme or topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how information from two or more books about the same topic or theme are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific claims the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the reasons in a text support the author's main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify information gained from visuals and words in the text, and explain how that information contributes to understanding of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find information from pictures and words in a text and explain how they help understand the story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain or describe, and how that purpose shapes the content of the text.
description_plain: Your child can tell what the main goal of a text is, like what the author wants to explain or answer, and how that goal affects what is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and describe informational text structures, including sequence/chronological and descriptive structures, and describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text and how they contribute to the overall structure.
description_plain: Your child can recognize how informational texts are organized, like in order or by description, and explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic words and phrases and how those words and phrases shape meaning in a grade-level text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words in a text and explain how those words help make the text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between individuals, historical events, scientific ideas or concepts or steps in technical procedures over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, ideas, or steps in a process are connected throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify implicit and explicit information from a summary to determine the central idea of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find both clear and hidden information in a summary to understand the main idea of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how, and make and support logical inferences to construct meaning from the text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how, and make smart guesses to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex literary texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use different reading strategies like asking questions and summarizing to understand and think about grade-level stories and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story written by different authors or from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a story's illustrations and words in print/non-print texts to demonstrate understanding of characters, setting and plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in stories or other texts to show they understand the characters, setting, and what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, acknowledge differences in the perspectives of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud, and how those perspectives shape the content of the text.
description_plain: With some help, your child can notice how characters see things differently and show this by using different voices when reading their dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how parts of the text contribute to the overall structure of poems, stories and dramas, including but not limited to linear, non-linear and circular structures.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of poems, stories, or plays work together, including stories that go in order or circle back.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases, including but not limited to regular beats, alliteration, rhymes and/or repeated lines, supply rhythm and shape meaning in a story, poem or song.
description_plain: Your child can describe how words and sounds like rhymes, beats, and repeated lines add rhythm and meaning to stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges in order to make meaning of the story development.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters react to big events and problems to understand how the story develops.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify implicit and explicit information from a summary to determine the author's message, lesson learned and/or moral, including but not limited to fables and folk tales from diverse cultures.
description_plain: Your child can find both obvious and hidden details in a story summary to understand the author's message or lesson, especially in fables and folk tales from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why and how, and make and support logical inferences to construct meaning from the text.
description_plain: Your child will ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to understand a story and make smart guesses about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a tricky word to figure out or fix how to say or understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally read grade-level text fluently on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books out loud smoothly and clearly after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read second-grade texts carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read fluently (accuracy, speed and prosody) on grade-level to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text accurately, at a good speed, and with expression to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot words that have unusual but common ways of matching letters to sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify, decode and know the meaning of words with the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and understand words that have common beginnings (prefixes) and endings (suffixes).

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel teams (two vowels that make one sound) in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: No Phonological Awareness Standard for grade 2.
description_plain: There is no phonological awareness skill required for your child in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: No Print Concepts Standard 1 for grade 2.
description_plain: There is no print concepts skill required for your child in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe degrees of certainty.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show how sure or unsure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child understands words better by learning their synonyms (words that mean the same) and antonyms (words that mean the opposite).

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context.
description_plain: Your child can tell when words or phrases mean exactly what they say and when they mean something different, based on the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words for everyday talk, school subjects, and special topics, including words that show location and time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries or simple dictionaries to find or check the meaning of important words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root.
description_plain: Your child uses a word they know to help figure out the meaning of a new word that has the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of a new word when a known prefix or suffix is added to a word they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the rest of the sentence to help understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written Standard English.
description_plain: Your child notices and follows the differences between how people speak and how writing is done in Standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child checks and fixes spelling by looking in reference books when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses common spelling patterns to help write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency words where suffixes are added to base words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly when adding endings like -ed or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child will capitalize the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use and choose the correct forms of describing words and words that tell how things happen, like 'bigger' or 'most quickly.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs agree and uses pronouns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use regular and irregular verbs properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make nouns plural, including those that don’t follow usual rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives and adverbs in a grade-level text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: When writing or speaking, demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and us-age.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and sentence structure when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose routinely over extended time frames and shorter time frames for a variety of tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on information from various print/digital sources, and analyze by sorting into appropriate categories.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from experiences, books, and the internet, take short notes, and organize the information into groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use digital resources to create and publish products as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers and other digital tools to create work and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a sense of closure that follows the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write endings that clearly finish the story or event.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation, and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that reflects linear, non-linear and/or circular structure.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by introducing characters and organize events in order, out of order, or in a circle.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child can write stories or texts that fit the purpose and task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives, using writing and digital resources, to develop real or imagined experiences or multiple events or ideas using effective technique, descriptive details and clear sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories, using writing and digital tools, about real or made-up events with clear details and order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to improve it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions to develop text structure across paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child can use transition words to connect ideas between paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate conjunctions to develop text structure within sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas within sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions and details.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, and details to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, group related information together, and use pictures to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child can write texts that are organized and fit the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative and/or explanatory texts, using writing and digital resources, to examine a topic and provide information.
description_plain: Your child can write informative or explanatory texts, using writing and digital tools, to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases that help connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons with elaborate details to support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child will give clear reasons with detailed examples to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic, followed by opinion statement, and create an organizational structure.
description_plain: Your child can start writing by introducing the topic, stating their opinion, and organizing their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child will write pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose opinion pieces, using a combination of writing and digital resources, on topics or texts, with supporting reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces using both writing and digital tools, giving reasons to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Legibly form cursive letters, words, and sentences with accepted norms.
description_plain: Your child can write letters, words, and sentences in cursive that are neat and follow standard forms.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can use different reading strategies to understand and think about challenging nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationship between information from two or more texts on the same theme or topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how information from two or more texts about the same topic is connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons and evidence support specific claims the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the reasons and evidence in a text support the author's main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain how specific visuals, including but not limited to diagrams, graphs, photographs and side bars, contribute to the meaning and clarity of a text.
description_plain: Your child can identify and explain how pictures, graphs, and other visuals help make the text clearer and easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own perspective from that of the author of a text, and describe how various perspectives shape the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own views and the author's, and explain how different viewpoints affect the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and describe informational text structures, including comparison, cause/effect and problem/solution structures, and describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text and how they contribute to the overall structure.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and describe how nonfiction texts are organized, like by comparing, showing cause and effect, or solving problems, and explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic words and phrases in a grade-level text, and describe how those words and phrases shape meaning.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words in a text and explain how those words help the reader understand the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between individuals, a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts or steps in technical procedures over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how people, events, ideas, or steps are connected throughout a nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and cite relevant implicit and explicit information from a summary to determine the central idea of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important information from a summary to figure out the main idea of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions, and make and support logical inferences in order to construct meaning from the text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text and make logical guesses to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex literary texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can use different reading strategies to understand and think about challenging stories and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast the themes, settings and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how the specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to an effect, including but not limited to creating mood, character and setting.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures and illustrations in a story help create feelings, show characters, and set the scene.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own perspective from that of the narrator or those of the characters, and describe how various perspectives shape the content of the text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own views and those of the story’s narrator or characters, and explain how different viewpoints affect the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe and provide evidence for how parts of the text contribute to the overall structure of poems, stories and dramas, including but not limited to linear, non-linear and circular structures.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of poems, stories, and plays fit together, including stories that follow different patterns like straight lines or circles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language, including but not limited to idioms and hyperboles, and describe how those words and phrases shape meaning.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words are used in a literal or a special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story, including but not limited to their traits, motivations, actions or feelings, and how they affect the plot.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters in a story by talking about their personality, feelings, actions, and how they change the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and cite relevant implicit and explicit information from a summary to determine the theme, lesson learned and/or moral, including but not limited to fables, folk tales and myths from diverse cultures.
description_plain: Your child can find important details in a summary to figure out the main message, lesson, or moral of stories like fables and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions, and make and support logical inferences to construct meaning from the text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story and use clues from the text to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Fluently read grade-level prose and poetry orally on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level stories and poems out loud smoothly and clearly after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books and understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read fluently (accuracy, speed and prosody) on grade-level to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts quickly, accurately, and with expression to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read words that don’t follow usual spelling rules for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify, decode and know the meaning of words with the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes, including Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and understand words with common beginnings and endings, including some from Latin.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: No Phonological Awareness standard for grade 3.
description_plain: There is no phonological awareness standard for grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: No Print Concepts standard for grade 3.
description_plain: There is no print concepts standard for grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand words by explaining words that mean the same or the opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings like idioms, adages, and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain what simple comparisons like similes and metaphors mean when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and how words relate to each other and have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions and that are basic to a particular topic.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use important academic and topic-specific words that describe clear actions or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation, and determine or clarity the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find how to say words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts like prefixes and roots to figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or nearby text to understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown words or words with more than one meaning by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between formal and informal discourse patterns based on context.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking or writing depending on the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child chooses punctuation to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when they write, speak, read, or listen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses a comma before words like 'and' or 'but' in compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to indicate direct speech and quotations for a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing speech or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of capitalization rules.
description_plain: Your child will use capital letters correctly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently confused words, such as to, too, two; there, their, they're.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right words when using commonly confused words like to, too, two or there, their, they're.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences and fix sentences that are incomplete or too long.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns.
description_plain: Your child understands how to put adjectives in the right order in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries to convey various conditions, such as can, may and must.
description_plain: Your child can use helping words like can, may, and must to show different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the progressive verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses verb forms that show ongoing actions, like 'is running' or 'was jumping.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns and relative adverbs.
description_plain: Your child uses words like who, which, where, and when to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: When writing or speaking, demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and us-age.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose routinely over extended time frames and shorter time frames for a variety of tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences, both over short and longer periods.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from various print and digital sources; take notes, categorize information and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can find important information from experiences or books and websites, take notes, organize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use digital resources to create and publish products as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses computers or tablets to create work and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing and rewriting.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, revises, edits, and improves their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes endings that match the story or events they tell.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that help others picture their experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of conjunctions and transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'then,' 'because,' and 'after' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses conversations and descriptions to show what happens and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that reflects linear, nonlinear or circular structure.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, organizing events in order or other ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes ideas well for the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives, using writing and digital resources, to develop real or imagined experiences or multiple events or ideas, using effective technique, descriptive details and clear sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories, using writing or digital tools, about real or imagined events with good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing and rewriting.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, and rewriting with help from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions to develop text structure across paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child uses appropriate words to connect ideas between paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate conjunctions to develop text structure within sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas within sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting, illustrations and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic and groups related information into paragraphs, using pictures or media when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative and/or explanatory texts, using writing and digital resources, to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts using writing and digital tools to share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing and rewriting.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, and rewriting with help from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-appropriate transitions.
description_plain: Your child uses appropriate words to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons supported by facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic or text, states an opinion, and organizes ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose opinions, using writing and digital resources, on topics or texts, supporting an author's perspective with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child writes opinions on topics or texts, using reasons and information to support their views.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, synthesizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child uses different strategies like asking questions and summarizing to understand and analyze grade-level informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two or more texts on the same theme or topic.
description_plain: Your child combines information from two or more texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular claims the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in print and non-print formats and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child understands information shown in pictures, charts, or videos and explains how it helps understand the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic.
description_plain: Your child compares a firsthand and a secondhand account of the same event or topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure, in a text or part of the text, the author uses to organize the events, ideas, concepts or information.
description_plain: Your child describes how an author organizes events, ideas, or information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a grade-level text, and describe and explain how those words and phrases shape meaning.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words in a grade-level text and explain how those words help us understand the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the individuals, events, procedures, ideas or concepts in a historical, scientific or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain who or what is involved, what happened, and why in a history, science, or technical text by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the central ideas are reflected in a text, and cite relevant implicit and explicit evidence from the text.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas in a text and can find clues in the text that show those ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, synthesizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex literary texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses different reading strategies like asking questions and making summaries to understand and think about challenging stories on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast themes, topics and patterns of events in stories, myths and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences in themes and events in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation, including making connections with what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can connect what they read in a story or play to what they see or hear in a movie or performance of the same story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast the point of view of first and third person narrators and the effect they have on the reader.
description_plain: Your child can tell how a story changes when it is told by a character inside the story (first person) or by someone outside the story (third person) and how that affects the reader.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the overall structure, in a text or part of the text, the author uses in poems, stories and dramas, including but not limited to linear, nonlinear and circular structures.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a poem, story, or play is organized, like if it follows a straight timeline or jumps around.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including but not limited to figurative language such as metaphors and similes, and describe and explain how those words and phrases shape meaning.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including comparisons like metaphors and similes, and explain how they help tell the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character's thoughts, words and/or actions, the setting or event(s) in a story or drama, drawing on specific details to analyze their interaction over the course of the text.
description_plain: Your child can describe a character’s thoughts, words, or actions and explain how they connect with the setting or events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the theme is reflected, and cite relevant implicit and explicit evidence from the text, including but not limited to poems, stories and dramas.
description_plain: Your child understands the main message or theme in poems, stories, or plays and can find clues in the text that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Fluently read grade-level prose and poetry orally on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level stories and poems out loud smoothly and clearly after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read fluently (accuracy, speed and prosody) on grade-level text to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts accurately, at a good speed, and with expression to help understand the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology to accurately read unfamiliar multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about sounds and word parts to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: No Phonological Awareness standard for grade 4.
description_plain: There is no phonological awareness standard for grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: No Reading Foundational standard 1 for grade 4.
description_plain: There is no Reading Foundational standard 1 for grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand words by knowing their synonyms (words with similar meanings) and antonyms (words with opposite meanings).

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of, figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition and other logical relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-level academic and subject-specific words, including words that show contrast or add information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation and determine or clarity the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses books or online tools to find how to say words correctly and to understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at common beginnings, endings, and root parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text, like cause and effect, to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers, slang) used in stories, dramas or poems.
description_plain: Your child can notice and talk about the differences in how English is spoken or written in stories, like slang or different accents.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or combine them to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language rules when reading, writing, speaking, and listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use strategies and resources (print and electronic) to identify and correct spelling errors.
description_plain: Your child can use books, websites, or other tools to find and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child knows how to show titles of books, movies, or songs by underlining, using quotation marks, or italics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no, to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence and to indicate direct address.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly with words like yes and no, in questions added to sentences, and when talking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can write full sentences and fix mistakes when the verb tense changes incorrectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states and conditions.
description_plain: Your child uses verb tenses to show when things happen, the order of events, and different conditions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions and interjections in a grade-level text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do in sentences.

description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
