description_plain: There is no print concepts standard for grade 5.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the connection between words to better understand what each word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including but not limited to personification, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language like personification when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of, figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands different types of figurative language and how words relate to each other and their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words to help understand and express ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation and determine or clarity the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child looks up words in books or online to find how to say them and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of words from Greek and Latin to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same style and tone when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentence styles to make writing or speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of strategies to identify and correct spelling errors.
description_plain: Your child uses strategies to find and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate use of punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses punctuation like commas and dashes correctly to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English and implement strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child notices when language is not standard and works to express ideas more clearly using correct language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and fixes mistakes when pronouns change incorrectly in number or person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize vague pronouns and correct pronoun errors, including but not limited to subjective, objective, possessive and intensive pronouns.
description_plain: Your child finds unclear or wrong pronouns and corrects them to make sentences clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: In both written and oral expression:
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose routinely over extended time frames and shorter time frames for a variety of tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences, both over short and longer periods.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and, in order to reflect or interpret, quote or paraphrase data and conclusions of others, avoiding plagiarism by providing in-text and bibliographic MLA or APA citation.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses quotes or summaries with proper credit to avoid copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital resources to create and publish products as well as to interact and collaborate with others; cite sources using MLA or APA format
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools to create and share work and to work with others, giving credit to sources properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With help, your child plans and improves their writing by revising, editing, or trying different ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending that fits the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, details, and descriptions that appeal to the senses to share experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words to show the order of events and to signal changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child will use storytelling tools like dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make events and characters come alive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tell events in a clear and natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child will write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or multiple events, memories or ideas, using effective technique, relevant descriptive details and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and a clear sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, re-writing, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ideas, focusing on the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will keep a formal tone in their writing when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to make the connections between ideas clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting, graphics and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and organize information using methods like comparing, defining, or showing cause and effect, and use pictures or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas in a way that fits the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative and/or explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative or explanatory texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, re-writing or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will keep a formal style in their writing when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrase and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to clearly show how their claims and reasons are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources, acknowledging opposing claims, and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, recognizes opposing views, and shows they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their claims and organize reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, synthesizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use different reading strategies to understand and analyze difficult informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast how two or more authors present similar events.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors tell about similar events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are sup-ported by reasons and evidence from unsupported claims.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments in a text, telling which claims have good reasons and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in print and non-print formats to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from books, videos, or other sources to understand a topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective and purpose in a text, and explain how it is conveyed in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view and purpose and explain how the author shows this in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how an author develops a key individual, event or idea over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author develops an important person, event, or idea throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the central ideas are reflected in a text by citing particular details and/or providing an objective summary.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas in a text by pointing out details or giving a clear summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, flexibly use a variety of comprehension strategies (i.e., questioning, monitoring, visualizing, inferencing, summarizing, synthesizing, using prior knowledge, determining importance) to read, comprehend, and analyze grade-level appropriate, complex literary texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use different reading strategies to understand and analyze challenging stories and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast how various forms or genres of texts approach a similar theme or topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different types of stories or texts handle the same theme or topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare/contrast reading a print text and viewing its visual/oral presentation.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story in a book to watching or listening to it and notice the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the perspective of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, chapter, or scene fits into the story and helps develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in stories or poems, including special meanings and sounds, and explain how word choice affects the mood or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes and determine how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the theme is reflected in the text by citing particular details and/or providing an objective summary.
description_plain: Your child can explain the theme of a story by pointing out details or giving a clear summary.

description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the story or poem to support what it says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

description_plain: Your child can tell the different feelings or ideas that words with similar meanings can have.

description: Use the relationship between particular words to improve understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including but not limited to allusions, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language, like comparisons and references, based on the context.

description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words that are right for their grade to understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.

description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze diverse media interpretations of a story, drama or poem, evaluating how each version interprets the source text.
description_plain: Your child can compare different versions of a story, play, or poem and explain how each one interprets the original text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze diverse media interpretations of a story, drama or poem, evaluating how each version interprets the source text.
description_plain: Your child can compare different versions of a story, play, or poem and explain how each one interprets the original text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how point of view and perspective are used to manipulate the reader for a specific purpose or effect, including but not limited to satire, sarcasm, irony and understatement.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author's point of view is used to influence the reader, including through techniques like satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how point of view and perspective are used to manipulate the reader for a specific purpose or effect, including but not limited to satire, sarcasm, irony and understatement.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author's point of view is used to influence the reader, including through techniques like satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s choices about organizing parts of a text affect its overall meaning and artistic effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s choices about organizing parts of a text affect its overall meaning and artistic effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone and on the text as a whole.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases in literature, including figurative language, and explains how word choices affect tone and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone and on the text as a whole.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases in literature, including figurative language, and explains how word choices affect tone and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices over the course of a text regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s choices shape the story or play and how its parts connect throughout the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices over the course of a text regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s choices shape the story or play and how its parts connect throughout the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more themes in a text, explains how they develop and connect, and summarizes the text objectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more themes in a text, explains how they develop and connect, and summarizes the text objectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important details from literary texts to support what they understand, including ideas that are implied.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important details from literary texts to support what they understand, including ideas that are implied.

subject: Math
description: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes in the world by building figures from components and drawing shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build and draw shapes to show shapes they see around them.

subject: Math
description: Describe the similarities, differences and attributes of two and three dimensional shapes using different sizes and orientations.
description_plain: Your child can describe how two- and three-dimensional shapes are alike and different, even if they are different sizes or turned in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional or three-dimensional.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a shape is flat (2D) or solid (3D).

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes regardless of orientations or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can correctly name shapes no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relative positions of these objects using terms above, below, in front of, behind and next to.
description_plain: Your child can describe where things are using words like above, below, in front of, behind, and next to.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes.
description_plain: Your child can describe things around them by naming the shapes they see.

subject: Math
description: Name and describe shapes in the environment.
description_plain: Your child can name and describe shapes they see in their environment.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and identify coins by name (penny, nickel, dime, quarter).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name coins like pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters.

subject: Math
description: Classify and sort objects or people by attributes. Limit objects or people in each category to be less than or equal to 10.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects or people into groups based on their features, with no more than 10 in each group.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/ "less of" the attribute and describe the difference.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects to see which one has more or less of something and explain the difference.

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes (length, height, weight, width, depth) of an object or a set of objects using appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can describe things like length, height, weight, width, or depth using the right words.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 using quantities (numbers with units) of ten ones and some further ones. Understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child understands numbers 11 to 19 are made of ten ones and some more ones, and can break these numbers apart and put them together.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number by using objects or drawings and record the answer with a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that makes 10 when added to any number from 1 to 9 using objects or drawings and write the answer.

subject: Math
description: Use objects or drawings to demonstrate equality as the balancing of quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use objects or drawings to show when two amounts are equal or balanced.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers into two groups in more than one way by using objects or drawings and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can break numbers into two groups in more than one way using objects or drawings and write or draw each way.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems and add and subtract within 10 by using objects or drawings to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction word problems using objects or drawings to show the problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds, acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction using objects, fingers, pictures, sounds, acting, talking, or simple math sentences.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers from 1 to 10 by looking at the written numbers.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group.
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group has more, less, or the same number of objects as another group.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions with as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 10 things that are spread out to answer 'how many?'

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions with as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 things arranged in a line, rectangle, or circle to answer 'how many?'

subject: Math
description: Given a number from 1-20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count out a number of objects when given a number from 1 to 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.
description_plain: Your child understands that each number name means one more than the number before.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.
description_plain: Your child knows that the last number said when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how they are arranged or counted.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object.
description_plain: Your child can count objects by saying number names in order, matching one number to each object and one object to each number.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers and amounts are connected when counting.

subject: Math
description: Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 to show how many objects there are, with 0 meaning no objects.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence within 100 (instead of having to begin at 1).
description_plain: Your child can count forward from any number up to 100, not just starting at 1.

subject: Math
description: Count backwards from 30 by ones.
description_plain: Your child can count backward from 30 by ones.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of or four of the shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts, call them halves or quarters, and understand that more parts mean smaller pieces.

subject: Math
description: Use three-dimensional shapes (cubes, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape and compose new shapes from the composite shapes.
description_plain: Your child can use 3D shapes like cubes and cones to build new shapes by putting them together.

subject: Math
description: Compose two-dimensional shapes to create rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles and quarter-circles composite shape and compose new shapes from the composite shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put flat shapes like triangles and squares together to make new shapes.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between defining attributes versus non-defining attributes; build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.
description_plain: Your child can tell which shape features are important to identify the shape and can build or draw shapes with those features.

subject: Math
description: Interpret data to answer questions about the table/chart that connects to the question posed, including total number of data points, how many in each category and how many more or less are in one category than in another.
description_plain: Your child can look at a table or chart and answer questions about totals, how many are in each group, and which group has more or less.

subject: Math
description: Organize and represent data in a table/chart with up to three categories.
description_plain: Your child can organize and show data in a table or chart with up to three groups.

subject: Math
description: Determine strategy for gathering data from peers.
description_plain: Your child can decide how to collect information from classmates.

subject: Math
description: Pose a question that can be answered by gathering data.
description_plain: Your child can ask a question that can be answered by collecting information.

subject: Math
description: Investigate questions involving categorical data.
description_plain: Your child can explore questions by gathering and looking at groups of information.

subject: Math
description: Identify the coins by values (penny, nickel, dime, quarter).
description_plain: Your child can recognize coins like pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters by their value.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Assign values to time and money.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use values for time and money.

subject: Math
description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of same-size length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end with no gaps or overlaps.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller objects of the same size without gaps or overlaps.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three objects in order from shortest to longest and compare two objects using a third object.

subject: Math
description: Relate the subtraction strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can explain how they subtract numbers using written methods and why it works.

subject: Math
description:
Subtract using:
  • concrete models or drawings;
  • strategies based on place value;
  • properties of operations;
  • the relationship between addition and subtraction
description_plain: Your child can subtract numbers using objects, drawings, place value ideas, math rules, and by thinking about how addition and subtraction are related.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 (positive or zero differences).
description_plain: Your child can subtract multiples of 10 between 10 and 90 from each other and get zero or a positive answer.

subject: Math
description: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find 10 more or 10 less than a two-digit number in their head and explain how they know.

subject: Math
description: Relate the addition strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used. Understand that in adding two-digit numbers, one adds tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
description_plain: Your child can explain how they add two-digit numbers by adding tens to tens and ones to ones, and sometimes making a group of ten.

subject: Math
description:
Add within 100 using
  • concrete models or drawings;
  • strategies based on place value;
  • properties of operations;
  • the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers within 100 using objects, drawings, place value ideas, math rules, and by thinking about how addition and subtraction are related.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100 including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number. Add a two-digit number and a multiple of 10.
description_plain: Your child can add a two-digit number and a one-digit number or add a two-digit number and a multiple of 10.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers by looking at the tens and ones digits and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight or nine tens (and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers like 10, 20, 30, up to 90 mean one to nine groups of ten and zero ones.

subject: Math
description: The numbers from 11 to 19 are composed of a ten and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child knows that numbers from 11 to 19 are made of one ten and some ones.

subject: Math
description: 10 can be thought of as a bundle of ten ones — called a "ten."
description_plain: Your child understands that 10 is a group of ten ones called a 'ten.'

subject: Math
description: Understand the two-digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones. Understand the following as special cases:
description_plain: Your child understands that two-digit numbers show how many tens and ones there are, including special cases like teens and multiples of ten.

subject: Math
description: In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers and show how many objects there are by writing the matching number.

subject: Math
description: Count forward to and backward from 120, starting at any number less than 120.
description_plain: Your child can count forward and backward up to 120, starting from any number below 120.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in addition or subtraction problems with three whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.
description_plain: Your child understands what the equal sign means and can tell if addition or subtraction sentences are true or false.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as counting on; making 10; decomposing a number leading to a 10; using the relationship between addition and subtraction; creating equivalent but easier or known sums.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20 and uses tricks like counting on or making 10 to solve problems easily.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 10.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Relate counting to addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child understands how counting relates to adding and subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction as an unknown-addend problem.
description_plain: Your child understands subtraction as finding the missing number in an addition problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child uses rules about numbers to help add and subtract more easily.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20, by using objects, drawings and equations with a symbol for one unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding three numbers that total 20 or less by using objects, drawings, or number sentences with one missing number.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart and comparing, with unknowns in all positions.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition and subtraction within 20, including problems about putting together, taking apart, and comparing.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes into equal parts and say what fraction of the whole each part is.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes that do not belong to a given category or subcategory.
description_plain: Your child can spot shapes that don’t fit into a certain group.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and classify quadrilaterals (rectangles, squares, parallelograms, rhombuses, trapezoids) by side lengths and understanding shapes in different categories may share attributes and the shared attributes can define a larger category.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and sort four-sided shapes like squares and rectangles by their sides and understand that some shapes share features that put them in bigger groups.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and classify polygons based on the number of sides and vertices (triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons and hexagons).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and sort shapes by how many sides and corners they have, like triangles and hexagons.

subject: Math
description: Classify polygons by attributes.
description_plain: Your child can sort shapes by their features.

subject: Math
description: Draw rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.
description_plain: Your child can draw rectangles that have the same perimeter but different areas, or the same area but different perimeters.

subject: Math
description: Find an unknown side length, given the perimeter and some lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find a missing side length when they know the perimeter and some side lengths of a shape.

subject: Math
description: Find the perimeter given the side lengths of a polygon.
description_plain: Your child can find the perimeter of a shape by adding up the lengths of all its sides.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems about the perimeter of shapes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as additive. Find areas of figures that can be decomposed into non-overlapping rectangles by adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child knows that the area of a shape is the total of its parts and can find the area by adding areas of smaller rectangles.

subject: Math
description: Use tiling to show in a concrete case the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths a and b + c is the sum of a × b and a × c. Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can use tiles or drawings to show that the area of a rectangle with sides a and b + c is the same as the area of two smaller rectangles added together.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can multiply side lengths to find the area of rectangles and use this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it and show the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of a rectangle by covering it with squares and knows this is the same as multiplying its side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Relate area to the operations of multiplication and addition.
description_plain: Your child understands how area relates to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square cm, square m, square in, square ft. and improvised units).
description_plain: Your child can measure area by counting squares that cover a shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.
description_plain: Your child understands that area is a way to measure flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Make observations from the graph about the question posed, including questions about the shape of the data and compare responses.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph and answer questions about the data, like how it is shaped or how different answers compare.

subject: Math
description: Show the data by making a dot plot where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units – whole numbers, halves, or quarters.
description_plain: Your child can make a dot plot graph using a number line marked with whole numbers, halves, or quarters.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch to collect data.

subject: Math
description: Identify a statistical question focused on numerical data;
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that involve numbers and data.

subject: Math
description: Investigate questions involving numerical data.
description_plain: Your child can explore and answer questions using number data.

subject: Math
description: Make observations from the graph about the question posed, including "how many more" and "how many less" questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph and answer questions like 'how many more' or 'how many less' about the data.

subject: Math
description: Create a scaled pictograph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set (using technology or by hand);
description_plain: Your child can make bar graphs and pictographs that use a scale to show data, either by hand or with technology.

subject: Math
description: Identify a statistical question focused on categorical data and gather data;
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about groups or categories and collect data to answer them.

subject: Math
description: Investigate questions involving categorical data.
description_plain: Your child can explore and answer questions using data about categories.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, or divide to solve one-step word problems involving masses or volumes that are given in the same units.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division with masses or volumes measured in the same units.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate masses and liquid volumes of objects using standard units of grams (g), kilograms (kg) and liters (L).
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate the weight and liquid volume of things using grams, kilograms, and liters.

subject: Math
description: Measure and solve problems involving mass and liquid volume.
description_plain: Your child can measure and solve problems about weight and liquid volume.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure elapsed time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals within and across the hour in minutes.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute, measure how much time passes, and solve word problems about adding and subtracting time.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions that have the same top number or the same bottom number by thinking about their size, and can use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and recognize when fractions are the same as whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions. Explain why the fractions are equivalent through writing or drawing.
description_plain: Your child can find simple fractions that are equal to each other and explain why they are equal by writing or drawing.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size, or same point on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands that two fractions are equal if they are the same size or point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions in special cases and compare fractions by reasoning about their size.
description_plain: Your child can explain when fractions are equal and compare fractions by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Represent a non-unit fraction a/b on a number line by marking off lengths of 1/b (unit fractions) from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size a/b and that its endpoint locates the non-unit fraction a/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show a fraction like a/b on a number line by marking off equal parts of size 1/b from zero and seeing where a/b lands.

subject: Math
description:
Represent a fraction 1/b (unit fraction) on a number line by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts.
  • Recognize each part has size 1/b.
  • a unit fraction, 1/b is located 1/b of a whole unit from 0 on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show a fraction 1/b on a number line by dividing the space from 0 to 1 into b equal parts and identifying one part as 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line; represent fractions on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands fractions as numbers on a number line and can place fractions on the number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a fraction a/b as the quantity formed by a parts of size 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that 1/b means one part when a whole is split into b equal parts, and a/b means a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 in the range of 10–90 using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply one-digit numbers by 10, 20, up to 90 using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 1000 using place value and math strategies.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 using place value.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table) and explain them using properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can find patterns in addition or multiplication tables and explain them using math rules.

subject: Math
description: Use various strategies to solve two-step word problems using the four operations (involving only whole numbers with whole number answers). Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, write equations with a letter for the unknown, and check if answers make sense by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100, using strategies such as the relationship between multiplication and division or properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100 quickly using strategies like the connection between multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Understand division as an unknown-factor problem.
description_plain: Your child understands division as finding the missing number in a multiplication problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to help multiply and divide.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing whole number in multiplication or division equations with three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use multiplication and division within 100 to solve word problems in situations involving equal groups, arrays and measurement quantities, by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about equal groups, arrays, or measurements using multiplication and division, and can draw pictures and write equations with a symbol for the unknown.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and demonstrate whole-number quotients of whole numbers, where objects are partitioned into equal shares.
description_plain: Your child can understand and show how dividing objects into equal parts works.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and demonstrate products of whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can understand and show what multiplication means with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
description_plain: Your child can find shapes that have lines of symmetry and draw those lines.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure.
description_plain: Your child can recognize a line of symmetry in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence of absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category and identify right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by whether they have parallel or perpendicular lines and by the size of their angles, and can identify right triangles.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse) and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw points, lines, line segments, rays, different types of angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines, and find these in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child understands that angles can be added together when split into parts and can solve problems to find missing angles by adding or subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles in whole degrees using a protractor and draw angles of a given size.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint and understand concepts of angle measurement.
description_plain: Your child understands that angles are shapes made by two rays sharing an endpoint and knows how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in dot plots.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with fractions using information from dot plots.

subject: Math
description: Make a dot plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8).
description_plain: Your child can make dot plots to show data measured in fractions like halves, quarters, or eighths.

subject: Math
description: Identify a statistical question focused on numerical data.
description_plain: Your child can identify questions that ask about numbers in data.

subject: Math
description: Use dot plots to analyze data to a statistical question.
description_plain: Your child can use dot plots to study data and answer questions about it.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Visually display measurement quantities using representations such as number lines that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can show measurement amounts using pictures like number lines with scales.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems that require converting a given measurement from a larger unit to a smaller unit within a common measurement system, such as 2 km = 2,000 m.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that change measurements from bigger units to smaller units, like kilometers to meters.

subject: Math
description: Solve measurement problems involving whole number, simple fractions or decimals.
description_plain: Your child can solve measurement problems using whole numbers, simple fractions, or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects and money.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to solve word problems about distance, time, volume, weight, and money.

subject: Math
description: Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table.
description_plain: Your child can write down measurement conversions in a two-column table.

subject: Math
description: Within any given measurement system, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can express measurements in bigger units as smaller units within the same measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship of measurement units within any given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child understands how different measurement units relate to each other in the same system.

subject: Math
description: Know relative size of measurement units (mass, weight, liquid volume, length, time) within one system of units (metric system, U.S. standard system and time).
description_plain: Your child understands how different units of measurement like length, weight, volume, and time compare within the same system, such as metric or U.S. standard.

subject: Math
description: Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or < and justify the conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain comparisons between decimals using >, =, or < symbols.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child knows that decimals can only be compared if they refer to the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to the hundredths place by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals that go to the hundredths place.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators of 10 or 100 as decimals.

subject: Math
description: Add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100.
description_plain: Your child can add two fractions when one has denominator 10 and the other has denominator 100.

subject: Math
description: Convert a fraction with a denominator of 10 to an equivalent fraction with a denominator of 100.
description_plain: Your child can change a fraction with denominator 10 into an equal fraction with denominator 100.

subject: Math
description: Convert and add fractions with denominators of 10 and 100.
description_plain: Your child can change and add fractions with denominators of 10 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems where a fraction is multiplied by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Understand a multiple of a/b as a multiple of 1/b and use this understanding to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying a fraction a/b by a whole number means multiplying the number of parts 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction a/b means having a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number using what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with the same denominator.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers when they have the same denominator.

subject: Math
description: Decomposing a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions.
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into smaller fractions with the same denominator in different ways and explain how.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands adding and subtracting fractions means putting parts together or taking them apart from the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction with numerator greater than 1 is a sum of fractions with numerator 1.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators using the symbols <, =, or >. Recognize comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Justify the conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different numerators and denominators using >, =, or <, but only when they refer to the same whole, and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b).
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying the numerator and denominator of a fraction by the same number makes an equivalent fraction.

subject: Math
description: Use visual fraction models to recognize and generate equivalent fractions that have different numerators/denominators even though they are the same size.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures to find and make fractions that look different but are the same size.

subject: Math
description: Understand and generate equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands and can create fractions that are equal even if they look different.

subject: Math
description:
Divide up to four-digit dividends by one-digit divisors. Find whole number quotients and remainders using
  • strategies based on place value
  • the properties of operations
  • the relationship between multiplication and division
Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers, find the answer and remainder, and explain how using place value and models.

subject: Math
description:
Multiply whole numbers
  • Up to four digit number by a one-digit number
  • Two-digit number by two-digit number
Multiply using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a four-digit number by a one-digit number and a two-digit number by another two-digit number, using place value and models to explain.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using an algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract large whole numbers using standard methods.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round large whole numbers to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digit in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two large numbers by looking at the value of each digit and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and break down large whole numbers into parts using number names and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare multi-digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare large whole numbers in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Recognize in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child knows that in a number, a digit in one place is ten times bigger than the same digit in the place to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern not explicit in the rule itself.
description_plain: Your child can create number or shape patterns that follow a rule and notice extra features in the pattern.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a given whole number is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a whole number is prime (only divisible by 1 and itself) or composite (has other factors).

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a given whole number is a multiple of a given one-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can decide if a whole number is a multiple of a one-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors.
description_plain: Your child knows that a whole number is a multiple of all its factors.

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a given whole number.
description_plain: Your child can find all pairs of numbers that multiply to make a given whole number.

subject: Math
description: Find factors and multiples of numbers in the range 1-100.
description_plain: Your child can find factors and multiples of numbers between 1 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Solve multistep word problems posed with whole numbers and having whole number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computations and estimation strategies including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, including problems with remainders, and check answers by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations in the conventional order when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order.
description_plain: Your child can do math operations in the correct order when there are no parentheses.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparisons by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that compare amounts by multiplying or dividing, using drawings and equations with a letter for the unknown number.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
description_plain: Your child understands that a multiplication equation can show a comparison and can write multiplication equations from words that compare amounts.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their features, like sides and angles.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape belongs to a big group, it also has the features of smaller groups inside it.

subject: Math
description: Represent real world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph in the first quadrant and explain what the points mean in real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use two number lines that cross at zero to find locations using pairs of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by joining two boxes by adding their volumes and use this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V= l × w × h and V = B × h for rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of boxes with whole-number sides to solve real and math problems.

subject: Math
